summary: Switzerland's posture is led by the National Cyber Security Centre, operating as the Federal Office for Cybersecurity (BACS), whose technology consideration and assessment frame the move to post-quantum cryptography as a long process needing planning in line with current standardisation. Federal work focuses on post-quantum cryptography rather than quantum key distribution, with a sector action plan for finance and parliamentary attention to the Confederation's own transition.
govActors:
NCSC (BACS) | Swiss National Cyber Security Centre, Federal Office for Cybersecurity, sets national post-quantum guidance
BIT (FOITT) | Federal Office of Information Technology, Systems and Telecommunication, urges an early switch for long-lived confidential data
SIF | State Secretariat for International Financial Matters, hosts the action plan for a quantum-safe financial sector
Swiss Parliament | parliamentary affairs on the Confederation's transition and on positioning Switzerland internationally
SNB | Swiss National Bank, reports adapting infrastructure to post-quantum requirements
